feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Business One
feed.subtitle: SAP Business One Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: recount Page Number in PLD for multiple customers range
entry.content:
Hi All

Is it possible reset SOA Page Count for each Customer Statement when print multiple Customers Range from AR Aging PLD?

 

Kedalene Chong

entry.title: How Growing Businesses Simplify Complex Operations with an Integrated ERP System
entry.content:
In the world of commerce, business growth often brings increased operational complexity. What once worked well for a small team—spreadsheets, point solutions, and manual processes—can quickly become inefficient and difficult to manage as the company expands.

For many growing businesses, this shift is gradual. What starts as a flexible setup soon turns into daily firefighting—reconciling data, tracking inventory manually, and waiting on reports from multiple teams.

As organizations grow, many leaders begin looking for better ways to manage operations, improve visibility, and maintain efficiency. This is where an integrated Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system can make a significant difference.

By connecting key business functions into one platform, an ERP system helps growing businesses simplify operations and gain better control over their processes.

The Business Problem: Disconnected Systems and Data Silos

In their early growth stages, companies often use different tools to manage various business functions. One software might handle accounting, another inventory, while a CRM manages sales activities—and operational data may still be stored in spreadsheets. Although this approach seems manageable at first, it gradually creates operational challenges that impact clarity and efficiency:

  • Disconnected systems – Departments work on different platforms that do not communicate with each other
  • Data stored in silos – Important information is spread across multiple systems and files
  • Frequent data errors – Manual data entry increases the likelihood of mistakes and inconsistencies
  • Overdependence on spreadsheets – Teams rely heavily on Excel for reporting, tracking, and reconciliation

Over time, what once felt manageable becomes difficult to control, often leading to confusion, delays, and limited visibility for decision-making.

The Consequences of Fragmented Operations

When business systems are not integrated, everyday operations can quickly become time-consuming and error-prone. While the impact may not be obvious initially, inefficiencies grow as the business scales.

Some common challenges include:

  • 1. Data duplication
    The same data is entered into multiple systems, consuming valuable time and often leading to costly discrepancies.
  • 2. Inventory mismatches
    Without centralized visibility, stock records may not match actual inventory—leading to stockouts, overstocking, or delayed deliveries.
  • 3. Slow decision-making
    Generating reports requires manual consolidation, delaying access to accurate insights when they are needed most.
  • 4. Process inefficiency
    Manual workflows create bottlenecks in approvals, reporting, and day-to-day operations.

These issues not only affect internal productivity but also impact customer experience and overall business performance.

Why These Problems Occur as Businesses Grow

As businesses expand, operational complexity increases naturally. A growing company must manage:

  • Multiple departments
  • Large customer and vendor databases
  • Expanding product catalogues
  • More complex supply chains
  • Higher transaction volumes

Without integrated systems, managing these moving parts through disconnected tools becomes increasingly difficult. Teams end up spending more time reconciling information than using it to make strategic decisions.

Eventually, many businesses reach a stage where they need a unified system that provides a single, reliable source of truth across the organization.

Signs Your Business May Need an ERP System

Many growing businesses notice operational strain as they scale. If your team frequently encounters the following situations, it may indicate that your current systems are limiting your growth:

  • Heavy reliance on spreadsheets and manual reporting
  • Difficulty tracking inventory accurately
  • Multiple software systems that do not communicate with each other
  • Lack of real-time visibility into financial or operational data
  • Increasing time spent reconciling data across departments

When these challenges start affecting efficiency and decision-making, it is often a strong signal that an integrated ERP system may be needed.

Before ERP vs After ERP

The difference between operating with disconnected systems and an integrated ERP can be significant:

Without ERPWith Integrated ERP
Multiple disconnected systemsSingle unified platform
Manual data entryAutomated data flow
Delayed reportingReal-time insights
Inventory discrepanciesAccurate inventory visibility
Limited collaborationCross-departmental visibility

How an Integrated ERP System Solves These Challenges

An integrated ERP system connects key business functions into a single platform, enabling organizations to operate more efficiently and with greater clarity.

Instead of relying on multiple disconnected tools, businesses can access real-time operational and financial data from one centralized system.

Key benefits include:

1. Centralized data management

All business information—from finance and sales to inventory and procurement—is stored in one system, eliminating data silos and ensuring consistency.

2. Improved accuracy

Automated data flows reduce manual entry, minimizing errors and rework.

3. Real-time visibility

Decision-makers gain instant access to accurate reports and dashboards, enabling faster and more confident decisions.

4. Streamlined processes

Automated workflows reduce bottlenecks and improve overall efficiency.

5. Better collaboration

Teams across departments can work with shared, up-to-date information, improving coordination and execution.

For many growing organizations, ERP becomes the foundation for scaling operations while maintaining control and visibility.

How SAP Business One Addresses These Challenges Across Industries

While the need for integration is common across businesses, the nature of operational challenges varies by industry. Solutions like SAP Business One are designed specifically for small and mid-sized businesses, helping them streamline processes while remaining flexible and scalable.

Here’s how this translates across different sectors:

Manufacturing: Improving Production Planning and Visibility

As manufacturing businesses grow, managing production becomes more complex. Common challenges include:

  • Difficulty aligning production schedules with available inventory
  • Limited visibility into raw material requirements
  • Manual tracking of work orders
  • Poor coordination between procurement, production, and sales

An integrated ERP system brings structure to these processes. With SAP Business One, manufacturers can manage bill of materials (BOMs), material requirements planning (MRP), and production schedules within a single system—ensuring materials are available when needed and production stays aligned with demand.

Distribution and Trading: Achieving Real-Time Inventory Control

For distributors and trading companies, inventory management becomes increasingly challenging across multiple locations.

Common issues include:

  • Mismatches between system records and actual stock
  • Difficulty tracking stock movement
  • Limited visibility into fast- and slow-moving items
  • Inefficient order fulfilment

With SAP Business One, businesses gain real-time visibility into inventory across warehouses, enabling better demand planning and more reliable order commitments.

FMCG and e-Commerce: Managing High-Volume, Multi-Channel Operations Efficiently

FMCG and e-Commerce businesses often face increased complexity as they scale across multiple sales channels, warehouses, and customer touchpoints.

Challenges include:

  • Inconsistent inventory data across warehouses and online platforms
  • Difficulty consolidating sales, returns, and financial data across channels
  • Stock-outs or overstocking of fast-moving products
  • Limited visibility into demand patterns and customer buying behaviour

An integrated ERP system centralizes data across all channels, enabling businesses to manage inventory more effectively, streamline order fulfilment, manage order returns, and make faster, data-driven decisions around demand planning, pricing, and promotions.

Service-Oriented Businesses: Improving Project and Financial Management

Service organizations such as consulting or IT firms often struggle with:

  • Tracking project costs and profitability
  • Coordinating teams and resources
  • Delays in invoicing
  • Limited visibility into project progress

With SAP Business One, businesses can integrate project tracking, financials, and resource planning—providing a clear view of project performance and improving overall control.

What to Consider Before Choosing an ERP System

Selecting the right ERP system is a critical decision that can directly impact how effectively your business scales. Beyond features, it’s important to evaluate how well the solution aligns with your current needs and future growth plans.

Key considerations include:

  • Scalability Can the system support your business as it grows in terms of users, transactions, and operational complexity?
  • Industry relevance Does the ERP offer functionalities that align with your industry processes, such as manufacturing, distribution, retail, or services?
  • Ease of use and adoption Is the system intuitive enough for your teams to use effectively without extensive dependency on manual workarounds?
  • Real-time visibility Does it provide accurate, real-time insights across finance, inventory, and operations?
  • Integration capabilities Can it connect seamlessly with your existing tools and future systems?
  • Implementation and support Do you have access to the right expertise to ensure smooth implementation and ongoing optimization?

Solutions like SAP Business One ERP are designed with these considerations in mind. Built specifically for small and mid-sized businesses, it offers a balance of functionality, flexibility, and scalability—while integrating core business processes into a single, easy-to-use platform.

Bringing It All Together

As businesses grow, managing operations through disconnected systems becomes increasingly challenging. An integrated ERP system addresses this by centralizing data, improving visibility, and streamlining processes across departments.

The result is a more structured, efficient, and scalable way to manage operations—without the need for constant manual intervention.

Is Your Business Ready for ERP?

If your current systems are creating inefficiencies or limiting visibility, it may be time to evaluate whether your business is ready for ERP.

At RPM Infotech, we work with growing businesses to assess their existing processes, identify gaps, and recommend the right ERP approach based on their specific needs. We combine domain expertise with a practical understanding of business operations to ensure smooth implementation, faster adoption, and long-term success.

Start with an ERP evaluation for your business—and take the first step toward more streamlined and scalable operations.

entry.title: Invalid password after resetting user's password
entry.content:
Hi All,
I have a user that keeps getting and error when trying to log into B1. 

They are setup as the below identify provider on SLD.

I have reset her password in the SLD and in SAP B1, making both the same password, but she still gets the error 

Not sure if I have to restart any services.  The rest of the users are linked via Active Directory. 
We are on FP2208, version for HANA.

Thanks all,
Jerusha

entry.title: SAP Business One Mobile cannot access DEMO system
entry.content:
Dear SAP Expert,

Can I ask if anyone know, if the SAP Business One Mobile DEMO system is offline?

Because I want to show to customer, but cannot access the DEMO system.

Thanks in advance for any help.

Regards,

Mat

entry.title: Who Close Purchase Order
entry.content:
Hi All

How is it possible to trace who Close a Purchase Order.

 

Kedalene Chong

entry.title: BK error using RSP
entry.content:
Dear community, I need your help. I've already asked my partner for help, but I'd like to hear your opinions on this error I'm getting when managing my scheduled backups using RSP. If I try to perform the backup using HANA Studio with an export command, I also receive the same error message. I tried reducing the number of threads, but the error persists, I am using SAP Business one 10 / hana. I've attached an image of the error message.

Thank you all very much.

entry.title: How to Mask data base in SQL database
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

We would like to ask for guidance regarding the proper approach for creating a development environment in SAP Business One.

Our planned process is as follows:

  1. Create a duplicate copy of our production database backup.
  2. Apply database masking/anonymization to all sensitive financial and confidential data in the duplicated database.
  3. Use the masked database as a development environment for our developers while creating custom modifications and enhancements in SAP Business One.

May we ask if there is a recommended or standard procedure for this setup?

entry.title: Approval Template issue
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

When we approve the document, it shows as updated. But when report and check, it still shows pending for approval.

Please guide.

entry.title: When modifying the purchase order, it prompted me that the tax data is missing
entry.content:
When modifying the purchase order, it prompted me that the tax data is missing
This is to generate a purchase order from a purchase application form, then split this order into two purchase orders because their warehouses are different, and then cancel the original purchase order, and add new purchase products to this purchase order.
My sap version is sap b1 10.00.120

entry.title: SAP B1 Crystal Report Stored Procedure Parameters Showing Incorrect Prompt Names After Deployment
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I created a Crystal Report based on a Stored Procedure with the following input parameter

IN FromDate DATE,
IN ToDate DATE,
IN BPCode NVARCHAR(50),
IN CurrType NVARCHAR(1)

In Crystal Reports, I created parameter fields with proper display names such as:

 

Everything works correctly inside Crystal Report preview.

However, after deploying the report into SAP Business One, the parameter screen displays incorrectly as:

 

Any help to identify the root cause or resolve this issue would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Alteração de codigo de imposto em lançamento advindo do modulo de venda futura
entry.content:
Boa tarde!!

A empresa que trabalha emite nota fiscal de venda futura e posteriormente envia nota de remessa para entrega do produto. 

Atualmente não consigo emitir uma nota de remessa vinculada a uma nota de faturamento simples ( módulo: vedas-> Venda futura) porque no momento de " copiar para - entrega" o sistema não me permite alterar o código de imposto. A nota de remessa deve fazer a contabilização descrita abaixo, pois a nota de simples fatura somente contábil Cliente (Ativo) contra a conta de simples faturamento (Passivo) e está correta devido ao formato de nossa operação. Minha pergunta é: Qual o parâmetro devo fazer para que no momento de copiar uma nota de faturamento simples (módulo: vedas-> Venda futura) o SAP B1 permita a alteração do código de imposto?

Dificuldades no processo:

1. No procedimento de copiar a nota de simples faturamento para entrega, o sistema não deixa alterar o codigo de imposto: Como liberamos o sistema para conseguirmos alterar o codigo de imposto na tela de entrega?

2.  A contabilização na Tela de entrega deve ser a seguinte: 

D - Venda para Entrega Futura (Passivo Circulante) 

C - Receita Bruta de Vendas (Resultado) 

C - Pis a recolher Passivo (Passivo) 

D – Cofins a recolher do passivo (Passivo) 

D - (-) Pis a recolher Passivo (Resultado)

C – (-) Cofins a recolher do passivo (Resultado) 

D - Custo das Mercadorias Vendidas - CMV (Resultado)

C - Estoque de Mercadorias (Ativo Circulante) 

Desde já agradeço. 

Atenciosamente

entry.title: SAP Business One Down Payment Reconciliation Error
entry.content:
Hello,

My client was posted a Down Payment Request with an amount of 23000 INR. And they paid for that downpayment amount with Outgoing Payment module. They create a A/P invoice for that vendor worth 15300 INR and also reconcile that Downpayment amount 15300 with that invoice amount. Balance 7700 INR was balance in Down Payment. That vendor paid that balance amount 7700 INR. Client was entered that amount directly in Incoming payment. Now they try to reconcile that Downpayment 7700 with Incoming Payment 7700 but it shows error 'A regular payment cannot be reconciled with a payment from a down payment request [Message 3821-45]'. Now what can i do. Pls anyone help me to close this issue asap.

entry.title: Crystal Report Parameters Automatically Mapped as From Date, To Date, Branch, and Cancel in SAP B1
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Is there any setting in SAP Business One to predefine or map Crystal Report parameters automatically?

I have defined the parameters correctly in both the Stored Procedure and Crystal Report, but after deploying the report in SAP B1, the first four parameters are automatically shown as:

  • From Date

  • To Date

  • Branch

  • Cancel

instead of the actual parameter names defined in the report.

Could anyone please confirm if there is any SAP B1 setting, cache, or parameter mapping behavior related to this?

Thank you.

entry.title: tax data is missing
entry.content: I found that when a purchase order is generated and there are two different warehouses in this purchase order, the system will automatically split this purchase order into two new purchase orders, namely Purchase Order -1 and Purchase Order -2. If some products in Purchase Order -1 are delivered and you modify the quantity or price of any product in Purchase Order -1, an error will be reported. It indicates that tax data is missing. However, after testing, I found that this situation does not occur when the data such as baseentry and base type of purchase order -1 is empty. Only by deleting and re-adding the product lines in purchase Order -1 can these base data be cleared. Are there any other ways to solve it?

entry.title: Who can assign SAP Learning Hub licenses: the CITP, the Learning Manager, or both?
entry.content:
Dear SAP Business One Partners,

We have received several questions regarding the assignment of SAP Learning Hub licenses, especially in cases where one person purchases the subscription, but another person in the organization is expected to assign the licenses to consultants or employees.

This usually creates the following question:

Who can assign SAP Learning Hub licenses to consultants or employees?

Is it enough to have the Learning Manager function assigned, or does the user also need to be the CITP / main contact for the Learning Hub subscription?

I am sharing the clarification below to explain the two requirements and the recommended approach.

entry.title: Price in pricelist empty, despite base price and no manual check.
entry.content:
Hi,

First time I have come across this behaviour. I have a price list based on another with a factor of 1.15 but there is no price for some items, even though there is a base price and the Manual check has not been set:

First I figured it was due to the affected item having or having had a Bill of Materials but the item in the screenhot is relatively new, and never had nor has now a BOM.

Is this normal behaviour for B1, and if so, how can I replicate this?

Regards,

Johan

entry.title: UDF Field Tab Order Navigation Issue in SAP Business One
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are facing an issue with the tab navigation order of User Defined Fields (UDFs) in SAP Business One.

Initially, during development, the UDFs were created in a different order without considering the final tab sequence requirement. Later, additional UDFs were added and some field arrangements were changed based on business requirements.

Now, when users enter data and press the Tab key, the cursor does not move to the next logical field. Instead, it jumps between UDF fields in an unexpected sequence.

For example:

Expected Order:
UDF1 to UDF2 to UDF3 to UDF4

Actual Order:
UDF1 to UDF4 to DF2 to UDF3

Since there are already existing records and the UDFs are actively being used in production, recreating the UDFs is not a preferred option.

Could you please advise:

  1. How SAP Business One determines the tab order for UDF fields?
  2. Can the tab sequence of existing UDFs be modified?
  3. Is there any supported method, SAP Note, or workaround to rearrange the tab order without recreating the UDFs?
  4. Has anyone faced a similar issue and found a solution?

Environment:

  • SAP Business One Version: 10
  • Database: SQL Server

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: SAP Bussiness One FAT-Client UI Display Issues
entry.content:
Hello everyone,
SAP Support suggested I try posting this in the community, since others here might have encountered a similar issue and could possibly offer a solution.

The issue is that for some of our customers—only sporadically and not reproducibly—the main menu gets pushed together in a strange way.

We’ve seen this with several customers using different SAP 10 versions.

We’ve already tested a few things, such as:

- Set the scale to 100% (still the case)

- Set the high DPI setting to System in SAP. (As with Corsuit)

- Disabled Windows multitasking. (Because errors such as the entire bar turning black occurred)

- Set the refresh rate to 60 Hz

- Installed the latest drivers (Today, we uninstalled all drivers on a test computer and installed the drivers directly from Intel)

- As silly as it sounds, all the computers where this error occurred were connected via HDMI cable. We even changed that to DisplayPort.

- What we found is that computers with, for example, an Nvidia graphics card do not have this error. Only Intel processors with integrated graphics cards (different generations 12/13/14) have this error.

-This error also occurred on non-Lenovo devices such as a Surface during a presentation where it was connected to a projector. Reports were open there (ID 42/2419) customer order, which was opened from the shipping list. When the packing list was opened, the error occurred. (Unfortunately, when testing with the same items on the device, this could no longer be reproduced).

-This error also occurred on non-Lenovo devices such as a Surface during a presentation where it was connected to a projector. Reports were open there (ID 42/2419) customer order, which was opened from the shipping list. When the packing list was opened, the error occurred. (Unfortunately, when testing with the same items on the device, it could no longer be reproduced).

-We also set up other monitors with only 1080p resolution because we thought it might have something to do with the screen.

-We also disabled settings such as hardware GPU acceleration in Windows.

-I also checked the system utilization and the network card, which were always only around 40% utilization. The Surface was the only one where it was at 80% at the time.

-I also changed the energy-saving settings to high performance or best performance on the notebooks.

-I also checked the network settings. I disabled Wi-Fi so that they are only connected via LAN. I also set the card so that Windows cannot disable it for energy-saving options. I also adjusted the other energy-saving settings that are available there.

-I couldn't see anything unusual in the Windows event log during that time.

Almost all of our customers use the Coresuite Country Package. However, Coresystems was unable to reproduce the issue on their end.

Maybe someone else has an idea, or has encountered a problem like this before.

Thank you very much and best regards,
Marco

entry.title: Hands-on Practice for SAP Business One - Logistics SLD iSSUE
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am using SAP Learning Hub and trying to complete the Hands-on Practice for SAP Business One – Logistics.

However, I cannot access or log in to the SAP Business One system that should be provided for the exercises. I am not sure whether this is a system issue or if I missed any setup steps.

Has anyone experienced the same problem? What should I do to access the SAP Business One system?

Thank you for your help.

entry.title: I want to modify the warehouse number that has already been used. How can this be achieved?
entry.content:
I want to modify the warehouse number that has already been used. How can this be achieved?
If this operation is not possible, is it only possible to create a new repository, then transfer the old one to the new one, and then disable it?
